office:text.text:p: The Expense Calculator & Manager is a comprehensive personal financial management application designed to help individuals track, categorize, and analyze their spending patterns. The application will be available across web browsers, Android mobile devices, and desktop platforms to ensure accessibility and convenience for users in their daily financial management activities.
Empower individual users to gain complete control and visibility over their personal finances through an intuitive, feature-rich expense management platform.

text:list-item.text:p: Individual users seeking personal finance management and budgeting tools

office:text.text:p: The Expense Calculator & Manager is a multi-platform personal finance application that allows users to record, categorize, and analyze their financial transactions. The application supports both expense tracking and income recording, enabling users to maintain a comprehensive view of their financial health. It includes budget management capabilities, recurring expense automation, and detailed analytics with visualizations.

text:list-item.text:p: Users must be able to add a new expense with the following mandatory fields:
Date (with time, defaults to current date/time)
Payment method (Cash, Credit Card, Debit Card, Digital Wallet, Bank Transfer)
Form validation must be performed client-side and server-side
Expense entry must be saved to the database immediately after submission
Users must be able to modify any existing expense
All fields must be editable except creation timestamp
Edit history must be logged for audit purposes
Users must be able to delete expenses
Deletion requires a confirmation dialog to prevent accidental removal
Soft delete mechanism must be used to preserve audit trails
Deleted expenses must not appear in calculations or reports
Users must be able to record income with mandatory fields:
Income category (Salary, Freelance, Investment, Bonus, Other)
Income entries must be clearly distinguished from expenses in UI
System must provide default expense categories:
Users must be able to create custom categories
Custom categories must support icons and color coding
Users must be able to edit and delete custom categories
Category deletion must be prevented if expenses exist in that category
Users must be able to set monthly budgets for each category
Budget can be set as a percentage of income or fixed amount
System must allow adjustment of budgets mid-month
Overall monthly budget must be settable across all categories
System must display budget utilization as percentage
Visual indicators (progress bars) must show budget status:
Alerts must notify users when approaching or exceeding budget limits
Users must be able to set up recurring expenses (e.g., rent, subscriptions)
Recurrence options: Daily, Weekly, Bi-weekly, Monthly, Quarterly, Annually
End date (optional, for limited recurring expenses)
System must automatically create expense entries on scheduled dates
Background job must run daily at midnight to process due recurring expenses
Users must be notified of automatically added expenses
This month, last month, this year, last year (quick filters)
Users must filter expenses by single or multiple categories
Filter must support AND/OR logic for multiple selections
Users must be able to filter expenses by amount range
Full-text search across expense descriptions and vendor names
Search results must be displayed in real-time as user types
Dashboard must display at a glance:
Quick stats cards with trends (up/down indicators)
Pie chart showing spending by category
Bar chart for comparison of top categories
Table view with category, amount, and percentage
Interactive charts with hover tooltips and legend
Line chart showing spending trends over last 12 months
Compare expense vs income over time
Customizable date range for trend analysis
Summary reports with key metrics and insights
Export all expenses to CSV format
Export filtered data (by date, category, etc.)
CSV structure: Date | Amount | Category | Description | Payment Method
Validation errors must be displayed with row numbers
Duplicate detection to prevent re-importing same expenses
Notification when category spending reaches 75% of budget
Alert when category budget is exceeded
Notify when upcoming recurring expenses are due
Notification when recurring expense is automatically added
Page load time: < 2 seconds for initial load
Database queries: Optimized with proper indexing
Database sharding strategy for large datasets
Automated backups: Hourly backups to geographically distributed locations
Disaster recovery: RTO 1 hour, RPO 30 minutes
Intuitive interface requiring minimal learning curve
Responsive design for all screen sizes
Clean, modern interface with minimal clutter
Emphasis on data visualization and insights
Consistent design patterns across all platforms
Primary Font: Inter (sans-serif) for body and UI
Secondary Font: Playfair Display (serif) for headings (optional, premium feel)

office:text.text:p: The dashboard follows a three-section layout:

text:list-item.text:p: Left side: Application logo and name
Right side: User profile menu (avatar), notifications bell, settings gear

office:text.text:p: Active menu item: Bold text with left border accent (4px #2E75B6)
Location: Right of sidebar (or full width on mobile)
Grid Layout: 12 columns, responsive (4 cols on mobile, 6 on tablet, 12 on desktop)
Layout: 4 stat cards in a row (2 cards on mobile, 2-4 on tablet)

text:list-item.text:p: Trend: Up arrow with percentage (e.g., "+5.2%" in green)
Icon: Money bag icon in top-right corner
Trend: Down arrow with percentage in green (good) or up arrow in red (bad)

office:text.text:p: Card styling: White background (#FFFFFF), rounded corners (8px), subtle shadow, padding 16px
Two-column grid layout on desktop, stacked on mobile

text:list-item.text:p: Legend below chart with category names and percentages
Interactive: Hover to highlight slice and show tooltip
Interactive: Hover for tooltip with exact values

office:text.text:p: Columns: Rank | Category | Amount | Percentage | Progress Bar
Progress bar shows percentage of total spending

text:list-item.text:p: Row styling: Alternate light gray (#F9F9F9) and white rows
Hover effect: Light blue background on row

office:text.text:p: Single-column form layout on all devices

text:list-item.text:p: Type: Number input with currency symbol prefix
Validation: Must be > 0, max 2 decimal places
Allow manual input or calendar popup
Type: Radio buttons or segmented control
Options: Cash | Credit Card | Debit Card | Digital Wallet | Bank Transfer

office:text.text:p: Buttons layout: Side by side on desktop, stacked on mobile
Display format: Card-based list (mobile) or table (desktop)
Columns (desktop table): Date | Vendor | Category | Amount | Payment Method | Description

text:list-item.text:p: Right: Amount in red for expense, green for income
Swipe left (mobile) to reveal delete and edit actions
Hover (desktop) to show action buttons (Edit, Delete)

office:text.text:p: Pagination: Show 20 items per page, with load more button or infinite scroll
Color: Green for under budget, yellow for warning, red for over

text:list-item.text:p: Horizontal progress bar with status color

office:text.text:p: Report type selector: Monthly | Custom Range
Summary section: Total income, total expenses, net balance
Category breakdown table with amount and percentage
Charts: Pie chart and bar chart of categories
Transactions table: All transactions in the date range
5 main tabs: Home | Transactions | Budget | Reports | Menu
Active tab: Highlighted icon and text
Floating Action Button (FAB) for adding transaction
Dashboard: Vertical stack of all cards and charts
Stat cards: Full width, visible one per row
Charts: Full width, optimized for portrait view
Sidebar: Hidden, accessible via hamburger menu

All development will be done exclusively using Claude Code with Python as the primary programming language. The following technologies are selected for their ease of integration with Claude Code and free/open-source availability:

table:table-cell.text:p: Lightweight, easy to deploy, perfect for Claude Code development. Free and open-source
Pure JavaScript for interactivity, no external framework dependencies required
Code sharing with web, free framework, Android support built-in
SQLite: Embedded, zero-configuration, file-based. PostgreSQL: Free, open-source for advanced needs

text:p.text:span: Free, open-source charting libraries with excellent browser support

table:table-cell.text:p: Standard, free, Python bcrypt library for secure password hashing
ReportLab generates PDFs, CSV export via Python built-in csv module
Free, open-source task scheduler for Python. Runs background jobs for recurring expenses
Local or free cloud (Heroku free tier, Render, etc.)
Can be deployed locally or on free tier cloud services for testing. SQLite for local persistence

text:list-item.text:p: Single-tier architecture optimized for Claude Code development
Python backend with Flask/FastAPI for REST API
SQLite or PostgreSQL for data persistence
Frontend: HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript (Vanilla JS or lightweight framework)
All third-party services are free or open-source alternatives

table:table-cell.text:p: Retrieve expenses with filters (date, category, amount)
Get dashboard metrics (income, expenses, balance, savings rate)
Get monthly trend data for charts (last 12 months)
Get spending breakdown by category for pie chart
Get all budgets with current spending status

text:list-item.text:p: Email/password registration with strong password requirements
Secure password hashing using bcrypt with salt
